body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-20T08:20:17</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>0</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>40</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>19/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>14/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>1</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>96</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>In Progress</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>4.3</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>4.3</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>6</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Moderate/Strong 41-60 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>N</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>None</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Excellent</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Sunny</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Mostly sunny. Winds northerly 30 to 35 km/h tending northwesterly 25 to 35 km/h in the morning.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Another stellar day!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p><b>Newsflash 8:15am update: Due to the awesome work of our Mountain Ops and Patrol teams Heavenly Valley is expected to open at some stage today once final checks are complete. Keep an eye on the Lift Report and Hotham App for live updates.</b></p><p>The extended run of stellar weather continues. At 6am I can see stars everywhere and the forecast is calling for another ripper day in the mountains.</p><p>With another temperature inversion last night, the current temp is +4.3 and could max out at +6 degrees later on. The wind has picked up a few notches and is currently moderate to strong out of the North.</p><p>Even though temps stayed above zero all night, the low humidity meant the snowmakers were able to get a window and they're still firing as I type. Just a reminder that as of today, scheduled lifts will revert back to regular operating hours - with the first lifts spinning from 8:30am.&nbsp;</p><p>The open groomed terrain will definitely be the best bet starting off firm with sunny aspects softening as the day warms. The cover on Heavenly Valley is marginal with shaded aspects likely to remain icy all day. Suitable for advanced and expert skiers only - if you venture out, be sure to ride with caution and observe all signage. The Extreme Area remains closed due do an extreme ice hazard. Do not enter!&nbsp;</p><p>Get out there for another fun one! Cheers, Burner</p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>19 July 2026
The cover is marginal and there is a very significant sliding hazard in the backcountry.   The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring-like turns as the day warms but shaded and south facing slopes will likely stay very icy and dangerous for the day.  Take Care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
